user_query:
Hi! I’m a chef who collaborates closely with a materials scientist to choose packaging that keeps ingredients fresh. I want to improve search visibility for three pages on my site focused on freshness-preserving packaging. Please use the local inputs and live web research to build a keyword plan and optimize meta tags, then produce the specified outputs.

Inputs (read and use all of these):
- Local site pages:
  - input/site/herb_storage.html
  - input/site/fish_packaging.html
  - input/site/spice_sachets.html
- Baseline keywords (per page): input/baseline_keywords.csv
- Materials scientist priorities: input/materials_priorities.json

What to do (make each step essential):
1) Read local pages and extract page topics:
   - For each HTML in input/site/, extract the page topic as the <h1> text. If no <h1> exists, use the <title>.
   - Also extract the full visible body text for later novelty checks.
2) Perform Internet searches (live, not cached) for each page using a general-purpose web search engine:
   - Build two queries per page, using the extracted topic:
     a) "{H1 text} packaging freshness"
     b) "{H1 text} best packaging materials"
   - For each query, capture the top 10 organic results with fields: rank (1–10), title, snippet, url, timestamp (ISO 8601), query, page_file (the local page path). Save all results to JSON at:
     - output/serp/{basename_of_page}_serp.json (array of the above objects)
   - Also write a log CSV at output/serp/queries_run.csv with columns: page_file,query,timestamp,results_captured.
3) Generate candidate keywords per page from three sources:
   - All baseline keywords for that page (from input/baseline_keywords.csv).
   - Any alias_keywords from input/materials_priorities.json that appear in the SERP titles/snippets for that page.
   - Additional phrases from the SERP titles/snippets (2–4 word lowercased phrases) that contain at least one of these anchor terms: ["packaging","freshness","fresh","shelf life","oxygen","moisture","barrier","film","pouch","wrap","laminate","desiccant","vacuum","MAP","aroma"].
   - Deduplicate within each page’s set.
4) Compute metrics and rank (filter, sort, and rank are required):
   - For each candidate keyword and page, compute:
     a) serp_occurrences: total case-insensitive count of the exact phrase across all captured titles+snippets for that page’s two queries.
     b) in_baseline: 1 if the keyword equals any baseline keyword for that page (case-insensitive), else 0.
     c) supported_by_materials: 1 if the keyword contains (case-insensitive substring) any alias_keywords in input/materials_priorities.json, else 0.
     d) novelty_across_page: "Yes" if the keyword does NOT appear in the page’s body text (case-insensitive), else "No".
   - Scoring formula (apply exactly):
     score = serp_occurrences + 3*supported_by_materials + 1*in_baseline + 1*(novelty_across_page == "Yes" ? 1 : 0)
   - Filter out any keyword with serp_occurrences = 0 unless in_baseline = 1 or supported_by_materials = 1.
   - For each page, sort remaining keywords by score desc, then by keyword asc for tie-breaks. Select the top 10.
5) Produce keyword artifacts:
   - Write a comprehensive candidate table for all pages to output/keywords/keyword_candidates.csv with columns:
     page_file,keyword,serp_occurrences,in_baseline,supported_by_materials,novelty_across_page,score
   - Write the ranked map to output/keywords/keyword_map_per_page.csv with columns:
     page_file,rank,keyword,score (exactly 10 rows per page unless fewer candidates survive filtering; include all that remain if <10).
6) Propose and apply meta optimizations:
   - For each page, choose the top-ranked keyword as the primary_keyword.
   - Create a meta title (40–60 characters) that includes the primary_keyword verbatim.
   - Create a meta description (120–160 characters) that includes at least one alias_keywords entry from input/materials_priorities.json relevant to that page’s content (case-insensitive substring match is sufficient) and mentions freshness or shelf life.
   - Output recommendations to output/meta/meta_recommendations.csv with columns:
     page_file,primary_keyword,meta_title,meta_description
   - Also write updated copies of the HTML files with the new <title> and a <meta name="description" content="..."> inserted or replaced under:
     output/optimized_site/{same_basename}.html
     (Preserve original body content; only change <title> and add/update the description meta.)

Verification constraints:
- Do not hardcode URLs. Use live search and record what you found in output/serp/*.json plus the query log CSV.
- The ranking must follow the scoring formula above and the anchors list exactly.
- The primary_keyword in meta_recommendations.csv must match the top-ranked keyword per page in keyword_map_per_page.csv.
- Meta titles must be 40–60 chars; meta descriptions 120–160 chars.
- Every deliverable path above must be created.

Deliverables summary:
- SERP snapshots per page: output/serp/*_serp.json and output/serp/queries_run.csv
- Keyword candidates: output/keywords/keyword_candidates.csv
- Ranked keyword map: output/keywords/keyword_map_per_page.csv
- Meta recommendations: output/meta/meta_recommendations.csv
- Optimized HTML copies: output/optimized_site/*.html

If anything’s unclear, please favor reproducibility and the specified structures over stylistic choices. Thanks! All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
